results.document:
[chunk 11]
Title: Benchmark Test-Time Scaling of General LLM Agents
URL: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.18998v1
Section: 

tools. These tasks emphasize structured tool calling and multi-step planning in realistic service and workflow scenarios.

Reason

For long-context reasoning, we use MathHay

(Wang et al.,

2024a

)

, which constructs queries by embedding relevant mathematical documents into noisy long-context haystacks. This benchmark isolates sustained reasoning over long inputs without relying on external tool execution, complementing the other domains.

Figure 2

:

Illustration of how General AgentBench covers a wide range of task categories while providing a unified interface to simulate real-world user interactions.

The green region indicates the specific task currently being handled by the agent (e.g., a search task). Orange boxes denote other clients and servers that remain active and responsive but are not directly involved in the current interaction. Red indicates that other domain-specific data are excluded.

Table 2:

Main results on

General AgentBench

. Benchmarks are grouped by domain.

Avg.

denotes the mean score across all available benchmarks for each model.

results.document:
[chunk 0]
Title: [2603.01557] Benchmarking LLM Summaries of Multimodal Clinical Time Series for Remote Monitoring
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.01557
Section: 

Computer Science > Artificial Intelligence

arXiv:2603.01557

(cs)

[Submitted on 2 Mar 2026]

Title:

Benchmarking LLM Summaries of Multimodal Clinical Time Series for Remote Monitoring

Authors:

Aditya Shukla

,

Yining Yuan

,

Ben Tamo

,

Yifei Wang

,

Micky Nnamdi

,

Shaun Tan

,

Jieru Li

,

Benoit Marteau

,

Brad Willingham

,

May Wang

View a PDF of the paper titled Benchmarking LLM Summaries of Multimodal Clinical Time Series for Remote Monitoring, by Aditya Shukla and 9 other authors

View PDF

HTML (experimental)

Abstract:

Large language models (LLMs) can generate fluent clinical summaries of remote therapeutic monitoring time series. However, it remains unclear whether these narratives faithfully capture clinically significant events, such as sustained abnormalities. Existing evaluation metrics primarily focus on semantic similarity and linguistic quality, leaving event-level correctness largely unmeasured.

results.document:
[chunk 1]
Title: Introducing RTEB: A New Standard for Retrieval Evaluation
URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/rteb
Section: 

ms, is fundamentally limited by the quality of search and retrieval. As such, accurately measuring the retrieval quality of embedding models is a common pain point for developers. How do you

really

know how well a model will perform in the wild?

This is where things get tricky. The current standard for evaluation often relies on a model's "zero-shot" performance on public benchmarks. However, this is, at best, an approximation of a model's true generalization capabilities. When models are repeatedly evaluated against the same public datasets, a gap emerges between their reported scores and their actual performance on new, unseen data.

Performance Discrepancy Between Public and Closed Datasets

To address these challenges, we developed RTEB, a benchmark built to provide a reliable standard for evaluating retrieval models.

Why Existing Benchmarks Fall Short

While the underlying evaluation methodology and metrics (such as NDCG@10) are well-known and robust, the integrity of existing benchmarks is often set back by the following issues:

The Generalization G

offer significant advantages in terms of

Falcon-H1-34B-Instruct

against

,

. These evaluations highlight Falcon-H1’s strength in scaling to longer sequences while maintaining high performance and efficiency.

results.title: Attention Residuals
results.document:
[chunk 0]
Title: Attention Residuals

### 한줄 요약
Transformer의 고정 residual accumulation을 depth-wise softmax attention으로 대체하는 Attention Residuals와 Block AttnRes를 제안한 Kimi Team 기술 보고서다.

### 핵심 기여
- PreNorm residual path에서 깊이에 따라 hidden-state magnitude가 커지고 레이어 기여가 희석되는 문제를 정식화한다.
- 각 레이어가 이전 레이어 출력 전체를 선택적으로 집계하는 Attention Residuals를 제안한다.
- 대규모 학습 비용을 줄이기 위해 블록 대표 표현에만 attention하는 Block AttnRes를 설계한다.
- Kimi Linear 48B/3B activated 설정에서 reasoning, code, Chinese benchmark 개선을 보고한다.

### 방법론
Residual sum 대신 이전 depth representation에 대한 softmax attention을 사용해 content-dependent aggregation을 수행한다.

### 한계
arXiv 논문이 아니라 GitHub technical report 공개본 기준이며, 재현 코드/체크포인트보다는 설계와 결과 보고 중심이다.
